executiveSummary.text: Risk-on, but narrow: S&P 500 +0.79%, Nasdaq +1.52%, Dow +0.26%; VIX fell to 16.45 (-6.8%). Equal-weight S&P (RSP -0.13%) lagged badly, so this is still tech/AI-led, not a fully broad cyclical upswing.

Rates are the main constraint: 10Y moved up to ~4.42%, 30Y ~4.90%, DXY ~101.17, while 10Y breakevens ticked to ~2.24%. Equities are absorbing higher yields only because AI earnings/capex momentum remains dominant.

AI/semis had the signal move: SOXX +4.30%, SMH +3.78%; KL holdings KLAC +8.38%, ASML +5.65%, LRCX +5.46%, TSM +4.94%, AMAT +4.08%, NVDA +2.63%. This reinforces the scarcity stack, but also raises valuation/positioning risk.

Commodities were mixed: copper +2.51% and silver +1.62% supported the electrification/scarcity thesis; oil was not confirming a new geopolitical shock (WTI -0.96%, Brent +0.27%); gold was flat despite higher yields.

Singapore was a rate-sensitive drag: STI-related news showed banks lower and Ascendas REIT -2.0%; KL holdings DBS -0.95%, OCBC -0.36%, UOB -0.35%, Sembcorp -1.53%, Ascendas -1.97%. No thesis break, but REITs remain hostage to long-end yields.

Base case 60%: liquidity/risk appetite stays supportive into early July but leadership remains narrow. 25%: rates/real yields force a valuation reset in long-duration tech and REITs. 15%: a commodity/shipping/geopolitical shock re-prices inflation and energy bottlenecks. Confidence: medium.

regime.label: Risk-on / AI-led, rate-constrained and narrow breadth

usGlobal.what: US indices ended higher, led by Nasdaq and semis; equal-weight S&P was negative.
usGlobal.why: AI capex/semiconductor leadership overwhelmed modestly higher yields, but participation was not broad.
usGlobal.implication: Risk appetite is real but fragile: KL should treat strength as confirmation of AI scarcity exposure, not as proof that every cyclical sleeve is de-risked.

ratesMacro.what: Yield curve stayed elevated: 2Y 4.10% latest FRED, 10Y around 4.42%, 30Y around 4.90%; DXY near 101.17.
ratesMacro.why: Markets still price a Fed that cannot ease aggressively while inflation expectations hover above target and growth/risk assets remain firm.
ratesMacro.implication: Long-duration assets need earnings growth to outrun discount-rate pressure; REITs/utilities remain vulnerable if long yields keep rising.

asiaSingapore.what: Singapore stocks weakened; Business Times reported STI down 0.7%, with DBS -1.0%, OCBC -0.4%, UOB -0.4%, and Ascendas REIT the worst STI constituent at -2.0%.
asiaSingapore.why: Local banks softened after prior strength and REITs remain sensitive to global yields.
asiaSingapore.implication: SG dividend sleeve still provides income ballast, but near-term price action is more rates-led than fundamentals-led.

commodities.what: Copper +2.51% and silver +1.62% rose; gold was flat; WTI slipped to ~70 while Brent was little changed.
commodities.why: Industrial/electrification demand and scarcity narratives held up, while oil market pricing suggested no fresh major supply shock despite shipping/Hormuz headlines.
commodities.implication: Copper strength has better read-through to KL’s AI-power-grid scarcity thesis than oil headlines today.

geopolitics.what: Hormuz/shipping risk remains a watch item, but oil did not price a renewed crisis in the last session.
geopolitics.why: Search results in the last 24h focused on improving shipping/resumption assumptions and demand softness, especially China, rather than acute supply interruption.
geopolitics.implication: Do not mechanically buy gold/oil on geopolitics; the chain to watch is shipping insurance -> freight -> energy prices -> inflation breakevens -> Fed/yields -> equity multiples.

moves.why: Part of broad semiconductor equipment/process-control rally as investors chased AI capex beneficiaries.
moves.implication: Positive for the semiconductor bottleneck thesis; does not signal thesis repair because thesis was intact. Risk is multiple expansion and crowded positioning.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD only on 10-15% pullback or evidence of sustained order acceleration; TRIM only if position size breaches risk limits or guidance deteriorates.

moves.why: Semicap/foundry complex repriced higher with SOXX +4.30% and SMH +3.78%.
moves.implication: 2nd order: hyperscaler capex flows to foundry/equipment; 3rd order: tighter leading-edge capacity supports pricing power but raises cycle/overbuild risk for 2027.
moves.action: HOLD; do not chase a one-day vertical move. ADD on earnings-confirmed backlog strength plus better entry.

moves.what: QQQ +1.70% and GRID +1.80% exceeded ETF abnormal-move thresholds.
moves.why: Mega-cap tech and AI infrastructure/grid beneficiaries participated in risk-on tape.
moves.implication: Supports KL’s “AI needs power/grid” thesis; second-order benefit to copper/electrical equipment names.
moves.action: HOLD/ADD on weakness; invalidator is AI data-center power demand slowing or grid capex cancellations.

moves.what: Ascendas REIT -1.97% and broader Singapore REIT pressure.
moves.why: Higher global yields and local rate sensitivity weighed on REIT duration assets.
moves.implication: Income sleeve is doing its job but will not rerate until long yields fall or DPU growth offsets financing costs.
moves.action: WATCH; ADD only if yields stabilize/fall and balance sheet/DPU guidance holds. Invalidate if refinancing costs force DPU cuts.

moves.what: Sembcorp -1.53% crossed single-stock watch threshold near the abnormal band.
moves.why: Singapore market weakness and possible utility/energy sensitivity; no fresh thesis-breaking news found in last-24h scan.
moves.implication: Noise unless accompanied by policy, power-price or project-execution deterioration.
moves.action: IGNORE/HOLD unless decline extends with company-specific news.

thesisImpact.what: AI scarcity stack strengthened in price action.
thesisImpact.why: Semis, equipment, foundry, QQQ and GRID all outperformed while copper rose.
thesisImpact.implication: Portfolio remains aligned with the market’s current profit pool: AI compute + power + bottleneck suppliers.
thesisImpact.action: HOLD core; rebalance only if sleeve concentration exceeds comfort.

thesisImpact.why: 10Y/30Y yields rose even as equities rallied.
thesisImpact.implication: Same yield move hurts REITs and can eventually compress AI multiples if earnings momentum pauses.
thesisImpact.action: Keep cash ballast; use dry powder selectively, not mechanically.

narratives.title: AI capex is still the market’s dominant marginal narrative
narratives.description: CNBC search result noted semiconductor leadership and small-cap first-half strength tied partly to AI spending; live price action showed semis far ahead of broad market. The market is rewarding suppliers to compute scarcity, not just app-layer stories.

narratives.title: Narrow breadth under the surface
narratives.description: S&P 500 rose while RSP fell. That is not bearish by itself, but it means index performance is reliant on the mega-cap/AI complex continuing to deliver.

narratives.title: Higher yields are not yet breaking risk appetite
narratives.description: 10Y and 30Y yields moved higher, but VIX fell and Nasdaq rose. The likely transmission is earnings optimism > discount-rate pressure for now; if earnings confidence wobbles, the same yields become a valuation problem.

narratives.title: Oil/geopolitical risk premium remains contained
narratives.description: Last-24h search results focused on shipping normalization/soft demand; WTI did not rally. The market is not pricing a fresh energy shock today.
narratives.source: Brave Search results: OilPrice/TradingEconomics/VT Markets, last 24h
narratives.priceImpact: neutral to disinflationary unless shipping stress returns

secondOrderEffects.text: Semicap rally -> higher KL AI sleeve mark-to-market -> higher concentration risk; 2nd order is more portfolio sensitivity to a single capex narrative, 3rd order is emotional pressure to chase NVDA despite deliberate underweight.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Let winners run but avoid turning a thesis into a valuation-insensitive bet.

secondOrderEffects.text: Higher long yields -> REIT price pressure and lower relative appeal of income equities; 2nd order is refinancing/DPU risk; 3rd order is delayed rerating of SG income sleeve even if operations are stable.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Use REIT weakness only when balance sheet and DPU coverage are intact.

secondOrderEffects.text: Copper strength -> confirms power/grid/data-center bottleneck; 2nd order benefits miners and grid equipment; 3rd order can feed capex inflation for hyperscalers, pressuring AI ROI narratives if costs outrun monetization.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Own bottlenecks, but monitor whether AI customers can pass through rising infrastructure costs.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil shock absent -> lower near-term inflation impulse; but if shipping risk reappears, path is freight/insurance -> headline inflation -> breakevens -> Fed pricing, not automatic gold upside.
secondOrderEffects.implication: React to rates and USD confirmation, not headlines alone.

investment.thesis: Maintain core AI scarcity stack: foundry/equipment/EDA/grid/copper over pure app-layer AI.
investment.trigger: Add only on 10-15% drawdowns, earnings-confirmed backlog, or capex guidance upgrades from hyperscalers.
investment.whyNow: Price action confirms leadership, but one-day spikes argue against chasing.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, foundry utilization deterioration, or evidence of AI compute oversupply.

investment.thesis: Keep cash/CPF/property ballast; do not dilute dry powder just because indexes are green.
investment.trigger: Deploy cash when VIX/yields/earnings setup creates forced selling rather than momentum FOMO.
investment.whyNow: Risk assets are strong but narrow and rate-constrained.
investment.invalidator: If real yields fall materially and breadth broadens sustainably, opportunity cost of excess cash rises.

trading.thesis: Avoid chasing semicap vertical moves today.
trading.trigger: Wait for pullback to rising moving averages or post-earnings reset; buy only if guidance confirms the move.
trading.whyNow: KLAC/ASML/LRCX/AMAT already repriced sharply in one session.
trading.invalidator: Fresh orders/guidance force a second leg with breadth confirmation.

trading.thesis: Relative trade: AI infrastructure over rate-sensitive REITs while 10Y >4.3-4.4%.
trading.trigger: Prefer GRID/COPX/semicap pullbacks over REIT adds until yields stabilize.
trading.invalidator: 10Y breaks lower with benign inflation and REIT DPU guidance improves.

watchlist.trigger: RSP and IWM outperform SPY/QQQ for several sessions while VIX stays below ~18.
watchlist.invalidator: Nasdaq leads alone while equal-weight keeps lagging.

watchlist.thesis: Rates danger zone for long-duration assets.
watchlist.trigger: 10Y sustains above ~4.5% or 30Y approaches/holds near 5% with DXY rising.
watchlist.whyNow: Equities ignored the yield rise today, but this can flip quickly.
watchlist.invalidator: Yields retreat with stable growth and inflation breakevens.

riskWatch.name: Narrow AI-led breadth
riskWatch.description: Index gains depend heavily on mega-cap/semiconductor leadership; RSP lag warns participation is not broad.
riskWatch.mitigation: Do not chase; rebalance only against risk limits; keep cash ballast.

riskWatch.name: Rates/real-yield pressure
riskWatch.description: 10Y around 4.42%, 30Y around 4.90%, latest 10Y real yield 2.16%; high discount rates pressure REITs and long-duration tech if earnings momentum slows.
riskWatch.mitigation: Add REITs only with yield confirmation; demand earnings support for tech.

riskWatch.name: Oil/shipping/geopolitical tail risk
riskWatch.description: Current oil price action is contained, but Hormuz/shipping disruptions could transmit via freight, inflation expectations and Fed pricing.
riskWatch.mitigation: Monitor Brent/WTI, breakevens, DXY and shipping insurance rather than headlines alone.

keyEvents.text: CNBC: June 30 US market live coverage highlighted semiconductor strength and small-cap first-half strength tied partly to AI spending.
Investopedia: Major US indexes advanced on June 30; 10Y Treasury yield was reported rising around the mid-4.4% area while oil slipped.
Business Times: Singapore stocks trended lower; STI down 0.7%, banks lower, Ascendas REIT down 2.0%.
FRED latest: 2Y Treasury 4.10%, 10Y 4.38%, 30Y 4.86% on June 29; 10Y breakeven 2.24% on June 30.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The market is risk-on but still rate-constrained and narrow. AI/semis are carrying the tape; rates are suppressing REITs and remain the main macro invalidator.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold KL’s AI scarcity stack, do not chase one-day semicap spikes, keep cash as dry powder, and only add REITs if yield/DPU conditions improve. Use probabilities: 60% supportive but narrow rally, 25% rates-led reset, 15% commodity/geopolitical inflation shock.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore generic “geopolitical risk = buy gold/oil” takes and daily SG REIT noise unless it changes DPU/refinancing assumptions.
